name: Q1985385
abstract: Nick Fatool (January 2, 1915 – September 26, 2000) was an American jazz drummer.

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Jerome
about_quotes.evidence: And so we went with him. Vernon Brown, myself, Nick Fatool, Les Robinson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Jerome interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: Who was on drums? Nick Fatool on drums. ... It was a good band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Nick Fatool in a parenthetical note as part of a small group, but the interviewee (Juan Tizol) does not mention Fatool by name, nor does he confirm any personal interaction or specific role with him in his response.

about_quotes.speaker: Johnny Varro
about_quotes.evidence: I got to play with a whole bunch of musicians I had never played with before, the West Coast guys. Nick Fatool, Eddie Miller, Dick Cathcart, Bob Havens, Abe Lincoln
about_quotes.doc_title: Johnny Varro interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Earl Watkins
about_quotes.evidence: Benny Goodman came up, by then Krupa and Harry James had left him but he had Nick Fatool on drums and Fletcher Henderson was playing piano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Earl Watkins interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Godfrey Hirsch
about_quotes.evidence: That was Mr. Tempo. Nick Fatool. Jack. Now, Jack's a good drummer. Jack Spurlin is a good drummer. But he's a great show off.
